name: Spanish B2 Vocabulary
description: Upper-intermediate Spanish — CEFR B2 level vocabulary

facts.explanation: abanicar — to fan (to blow air on something by means of a fan).
abanico — fan (hand-held device). Also: range (selection, array)
abrasar — to burn. Also: to parch, to squander or waste money
abundar — to abound. Also: to satisfy
acariciar — to caress. Also: to fondle, to stroke, to pet
acelerar — to accelerate (to cause to move faster). Also: to hurry up
acentuar — to accentuate (to pronounce with vocal stress). Also: to accentuate (to mark with a written accent), to accentuate, to emphasize
acero — steel. Also: sword, weapon, arms, tincture of steel
acertar — to hit, to hit upon. Also: to be right
acierto — correct answer. Also: being correct, being right; correctness, success, hit
acogedor — cozy. Also: friendly, inviting, welcoming, hospitable
acoger — to receive, welcome. Also: to take in, to host (an event)
acompañamiento — the act of accompanying; accompaniment. Also: the accompanying part; accompaniment, side dish
acudir — to go, come [with a ‘to’], to attend, visit. Also: to turn to, consult, resort, to apply, appeal [with a ‘to’] (a court, government department, commission, etc.), to come to mind
acupuntura — acupuncture (insertion of needles for remedial purposes).

adelantar — to bring forward, advance, to move forward (in space or time). Also: to pay in advance, to outstrip, to outrun, to make progress, to overtake, to pass (to pass a more slowly moving object), to move up, speed up (to make something happen faster or come sooner), to fast forward (e.g. a film or recording), to preempt, to anticipate, to forestall (+ a), to get ahead of, to stay ahead of (+ a), to jump the gun, get ahead of oneself, to be brought forward
adicción — addiction (compulsive usage). Also: addiction (devotion; inclination)
adjuntar — to enclose, to attach (as in correspondence, e-mail).

administración — administration (act of administering government or public affairs). Also: administration (a body that administers), administration, administering (act of administering something to another)
administrador — administrator (one who administers affairs). Also: administrator (one who is responsible for software management)
administrativo — administrative (of or relating to administering or administration).
admitir — to accept, to admit, to agree to, to concede. Also: to admit, to let in, to permit, to allow
adrenalina — adrenaline (epinephrine, the hormone and neurotransmitter).
advertir — to warn. Also: to notice
afecto — prone; affected. Also: affected (by a disease)

afrontar — to face, address, tackle, meet, deal with, confront, cope with, handle, bear, manage, (e.g. reality, an issue, a situation, a problem). Also: to defy, to brave, to afford, to bear (e.g. a cost, a loss)
agravar — to aggravate. Also: to get worse, to aggro.
agresión — aggression (the act of initiating hostilities). Also: aggression (hostile or destructive behavior or actions)

aguantar — to hold, withhold, hold back, retain (breath, urine). Also: to put up with, to tolerate or bear, to endure, to handle, to take, to stand, to hang on, to grasp, to hang on, to persevere, to last, to not complain, to keep silent, to hold (a posture)

agudo — sharp, pointed, acute (terminating in a point or edge, especially one that can cut easily). Also: acute, sharp, witty, keen (intelligent), sharp, stabbing, acute, severe, acute (sudden and intense), acute, sharp (sensitive), high, high-pitched, shrill, sharp, piercing, acute, having the voiced accent on the last syllable, acute (less than 90 degrees)
aislamiento — isolation. Also: insulation, solitary confinement
ajustar — to adjust, to set. Also: to fine-tune, to fit, to conform to, abide by, act in accordance with, to adjust (oneself/itself)
alfarería — pottery workshop. Also: pottery craft; earthenware
alimentario — alimentary, dietary, food, food-related. Also: eating
almohada — pillow (soft cushion used to support the head).
alto — tall. Also: high, loud, upper, top, senior (rank)
alucinar — to hallucinate. Also: to fascinate
alumnado — student body, populace of students.
alzar — to lift, raise. Also: to remove; to steal, to hide, to rise up, revolt, to achieve, obtain, to appeal
amasar — to knead. Also: to amass (accumulate)

ampliar — to extend, expand, enlarge. Also: to broaden, to widen, to further, to elaborate on, to expand on, to magnify, to blow up, to enlarge, to scale up (e.g. a photograph, a model), to expand, to extend (itself, oneself), to be extended, to be expanded, to be broadened, to be enlarged, to be widened, to be increased, to be scaled up
angustia — anguish, agony. Also: anxiety, distress
animación — animation. Also: jauntiness; liveliness, cheerleading
anular — to annul. Also: to cancel, call off, to vanish
análisis — analysis (decomposition into parts). Also: analysis (the result of such a decomposition), analysis
aparición — appearance (act of appearing). Also: appearance (a thing seen), apparition
apoderar — to empower. Also: to seize, to gain, to get hold of, to get one's hands on, to take control, to take possession
aportar — to contribute. Also: to provide; to give, to bring, to dock, to enter into a port
aptitud — aptitude, ability (inherent). Also: suitability, competence, proficiency, skill, capacity (acquired), fitness
arco — bow (weapon). Also: bow (rod for an instrument), arc, arch, goal (structure)

arder — to burn, blaze (to be consumed by fire or in flames). Also: to burn, to sting, to smart (to hurt, be in pain), to burn, seethe (to be in an agitated or angry mental state), to rot (to suffer decomposition due to biological action), to cause someone resentment or irritation, to get resentful, offended
argumentar — to argue (to make a case for a point of view). Also: to dispute
arrastrar — to drag. Also: to pull, to tow, to haul, to incite, to sweep away (e.g., a river), to lug, to drag along, to drag down, to crawl, to creep, to drag oneself, to grovel
arrebatar — to snatch, wrest. Also: to captivate (with beauty), to take (a life), to get angry, to get carried away
arrepentir — only used in se ... arrepentir, syntactic variant of arrepentirse.
arriesgar — to venture; to risk; to hazard. Also: to endanger; to put at risk, to stake

arrojar — to throw, to hurl, to toss, to fling (cause an object to move rapidly through the air). Also: to emit (to send out or give off), to eject, to kick out (to compel (a person or persons) to leave), to yield, to produce; to shed (light) on, to vomit, to cast (doubt on)
asar — to roast. Also: to grill, to pester, to plague, to be roasting, to feel very hot
asco — disgust. Also: nausea, disgusting person
asemejar — to make similar. Also: to be similar, resemble
asesorar — to advise. Also: give legal advice to; give professional advice to, to seek advice
asignatura — class, subject (particular area of study).
asombro — amazement, astonishment, stupefaction. Also: wonder, awe, wonderment, fear, dread, amazing thing, dread or astonishing person or thing, prodigy
aspiración — dream, wish, aspiration. Also: aspiration, suction
aspirar — to inhale. Also: to aspire, to desire to be, to vacuum
astuto — cunning, crafty, sly, wily, slick, foxy, artful. Also: astute, shrewd, smart, clever, canny, sneaky
atardecer — to draw towards evening, to grow late, to get dark.
atribuir — to assign. Also: to ascribe to, to attribute to, to chalk up to (+ a)
avance — advance. Also: progress, advance payment, preview, trailer
bachillerato — graduation certificate (degree from high school). Also: ellipsis of bachillerato universitario (bachelor’s degree), eleventh and twelfth grade
bagazo — bagasse (sugarcane pulp). Also: grape residue, marc, a despicable person
banca — small bench. Also: seat (in an elective body), financial system, a kind of large pitcher made of coarse clay
bandeja — tray, platter (small, typically rectangular or round, flat, and rigid object upon which things are carried).
barrera — barrier. Also: wall (line of players standing side-by-side to block a free kick)
bautizar — to baptize. Also: to name (to give a name to)
biblia — bible (a comprehensive manual that describes something). Also: a wall of text
blando — soft. Also: bland, flabby, cowardly, flat, softcore
blanquear — to bleach. Also: to whiten; to turn white, to whitewash, to win without allowing rival to get points
boom — a boom (sound). Also: a boom (rapid expansion or increase), boom (period of prosperity)
brillo — shine, glow, radiance. Also: brightness, brilliance, shininess, éclat, sheen, luster, gleam, glitter, gloss, sparkle, glistening, glint

burlar — to outwit, to outsmart, to circumvent, to trick, to deceive. Also: to circumvent, to evade, to cheat, to make fun of, to poke fun at, to mock, to ridicule, to jeer (+ de), to taunt, to tease (often uses de), to scoff, to scoff at (+ de), to deceive oneself (+ de)
burro — donkey, especially one used as a pack animal. Also: a dunce, an idiot, a well-hung man, sawhorse, old maid (card game), burrito, Aloysia polystachya
busca — search (an attempt to find something).
cabecera — headboard, bedhead. Also: head (of company), seat of government, headline, heading, top, opening credits, intro, title sequence, headwaters, header

calabaza — pumpkin, Cucurbita pepo (a typical pumpkin used for Halloween and autumn seasonal decoration). Also: gourd, pumpkin, cep, porcino (Boletus edulis), ellipsis of calabaza de nuez; butternut squash
calificar — to qualify. Also: to grade, to mark, to rate, to score, to describe, to characterize, to label, to categorize, to designate (e.g. designate a plot of land for a specific use)
cancha — pitch, (US) field (field on which soccer, rugby or field hockey is played). Also: course, court (field on which tennis, basketball, handball, badminton, etc is played), enclosure, shed
capacitar — to prepare. Also: to train
captar — to capture, perceive, register, understand.
carecer — to lack, to be lacking.
carga — load. Also: burden, cargo, charge, carga (a traditional unit of dry measure equivalent to about 222 L), blitz
cargo — charge, burden. Also: position, post, debit, charge
carroza — carriage. Also: state carriage, hearse (vehicle for taking dead body to grave)

castigar — to punish, to castigate (to cause to suffer for crime or misconduct). Also: to ground, gate (to punish, especially a child or teenager, by forcing them to stay at home and/or give up certain privileges)
ceja — eyebrow. Also: rim, edge, fret, capotasto, clearing (in the woods, in clouds, etc.)
certamen — contest, competition, pageant. Also: challenge
cesar — to cease (to stop). Also: to quit, to step down, to dismiss
chillón — flashy, bright. Also: squeaky, loud; rambunctious, crybabyish; weepy, cowardly
cierre — shutting, closing. Also: shutting device, fastener; buckle, clasp, shutter, blind, zipper
cima — top. Also: peak, summit, mountaintop (top of a mountain or hill), cyme
cimiento — foundation (the lowest and supporting layer of a superstructure). Also: foundation, groundwork (the result of the work to begin something)
cineasta — filmmaker, moviemaker (a producer or director of films/movies).
cinturón — belt. Also: cincture, seat belt
citar — to cite. Also: to summon, to schedule, make an appointment
clavar — to nail, to pin, to stick.
cojo — lame, crippled. Also: lame, defective, shaky
colar — to sift, to strain, to filter. Also: to prepare coffee, to dupe, hoodwink, to missay, say wrongly, to fall for, fall in love, to sneak into, to crash, to sift through, comb through
comercializar — to sell, commercialize, market. Also: to go on the market; to enter the market (become available for sale)
comisión — committee. Also: commission (fee charged for carrying out a transaction), commission (act of committing), commission, task, homework
competencia — competence, expertise. Also: jurisdiction, purview, competition
complejo — complex. Also: complex, compound (collection of buildings or facilities), resort, complex (where one can vacation to), hang-up
comportar — to involve. Also: to tolerate, to entail, to behave, to act, to behave oneself
concertar — to arrange, to set up. Also: to agree
concha — seashell. Also: shell (mollusk), a type of sweet bread (one that resembles a shell in design and in decoration), pussy
concluir — to conclude. Also: to overwhelm, to silence
conclusión — conclusion, end. Also: completion, finding, bottom line
conformar — to form, constitute. Also: to conform to (+ con), to be satisfied with, to make do with (+ con)
confundir — to confuse, to throw off, to baffle, to perplex. Also: to mistake, to confuse, to mix up (misidentify), to confound
congelar — to freeze (to lower something's temperature to the point that it freezes). Also: to freeze, to suspend (an activity, a program)
conllevar — to entail, involve, present, go with. Also: to assist someone who is in trouble, to suffer
consciente — aware, cognizant. Also: conscious, mindful
contactar — to contact, to reach out to.

continuo — continual, constant (done or extending without interruption). Also: continuous, adjacent (extending from one to another without interruption), steady, persevering (continuing with one's task without interruption), continual, nondiscrete (existing in an uninterrupted continuum)
contundente — heavy, severe. Also: blunt, overwhelming, resounding, hearty (promoting strength; nourishing)
convenio — agreement. Also: covenant, convention (an international agreement)
convocar — to call, convene, convoke. Also: to call up, enlist
convocatoria — call (beckoning or summoning). Also: announcement, round (of exams or voting)
corporal — body; corporal. Also: bodywide or systemic
corteza — bark (of a tree). Also: rind, peel (of a fruit), crust (of food or geologically)
costear — to afford. Also: to pay for; to finance
creyente — believing. Also: religious, pious, devout
criterio — criterion. Also: capacity to judge or take a decision

crucero — cruise (a sea or lake voyage, especially one taken for pleasure). Also: cruise ship, cruiser (a passenger ship used for pleasure voyages, where the voyage itself and the ship's amenities are considered an essential part of the experience), cruiser (a class of fast warships of medium tonnage, having a long cruising range but less armour and firepower than a battleship), crossing (the volume formed by the intersection of chancel, nave and transepts in a cruciform church; often with a tower or cupola over it), crossing, crossroads, intersection (an intersection where roads cross), calvary (a type of monumental public Christian cross, sometimes encased in an open shrine)
cría — kid, pup, cria. Also: litter, childrearing, breeding, rearing of an animal
cuanto — as much; however much; in whatever quantity.

cubierta — cover, covering, casing, lid, shroud. Also: cover, jacket (of a book), envelope (i.e. any natural covering), sheath (a structure covering an animal or plant organ), sheath (for cables), roof (exterior part), tire, hood (e.g. hood for a pipe or a fish tank), shell (e.g. for capsules), cover (pretext, false background), deck
cuerno — horn. Also: croissant, sexual or marital infidelity, AK-47
cueva — cave. Also: basement, cellar, black-market bureau de change; a place to exchange pesos for dólares blue
currículo — curriculum (set of courses and content offered by an educational institution). Also: CV; résumé; curriculum vitae (written account of one's education, accomplishments etc.)

facts.correctAnswer: to date, to assign a date
facts.explanation: datar — to date, assign a date to. Also: to date
dañar — to damage, to harm, to hurt, to injure. Also: to condemn, to hex, curse
debatir — to debate. Also: to struggle (in a decision)
decepcionar — to disappoint, to let down, to underwhelm.
decisivo — decisive. Also: defining (e.g. a defining moment), crucial, critical, key, instrumental, vital, deciding, swing

definición — definition (a statement of the meaning of a word). Also: definition (a clear instance conforming to the dictionary or textbook definition), definition (clarity of visual presentation, distinctness of outline or detail), rules, statutes
defraudar — to defraud. Also: to dodge, to disappoint
delicado — delicate. Also: fragile, dainty, fine, refined, fussy, subtle (flavor, color), effeminate, gay
demandar — to insist, ask (for something), request. Also: to sue, file suit, implead, to take charge (of something)
denotar — to show. Also: to denote
depurar — to purify. Also: to debug

derribar — to bring down, to shoot down. Also: to knock down, to demolish, to break down, to tear down, to topple, to pull down, to kick down, to batter down, to overthrow, to take down, to tackle, to knock over
derrota — path, route, track. Also: course
descartar — to rule out, to discard, to discount, to dismiss, to reject. Also: to discard
desembocar — to culminate, conclude. Also: to flow out
desempeñar — to hold (a position, a title). Also: to play a role; to serve in or as, to redeem (out of pawn)
desgraciar — to disgrace. Also: to ruin, to punish, to seriously injure or kill someone, to damage or spoil
desordenar — to mess up; to make less tidy.
despistar — to throw off track. Also: to disorient, to get distracted, to fall asleep
desplazamiento — displacement. Also: offset, shift, motion, scrolling
desplazar — to displace. Also: to move
destapar — to remove the lid, open, uncork. Also: to discover, uncover, to reveal a political candidate
deteriorar — to ruin, spoil. Also: to worsen, deteriorate

determinación — determination, identification, establishment, establishing (i.e. figuring out something with relative certainty). Also: determination, resolve, resoluteness (i.e. firmness of purpose or intention), decision, setting, fixing, determining (e.g. fixing of a deadline, setting of objectives or goals), firmness
deuda — debt (an obligation to perform for another). Also: debt (the state of owing something to another), debt (money that someone owes to another)
dificultar — to make difficult, to hamper, to hinder, to impede, to complicate.
difusión — dissemination, spreading, spread, diffusion, media.
diputado — deputy. Also: a representative in a legislative chamber
directiva — board of directors. Also: female equivalent of directivo, female director or executive
directivo — director; directing (related to the managing of a department or company).
disciplina — discipline (control). Also: discipline, subject (area of study), disciplina (a hemp whip similar to the cat-o'-nine-tails)
disparar — to shoot, to fire (e.g. a weapon, a rocket). Also: to rise, to go up, to skyrocket, to go off, to be suddenly jolted into action
disputa — dispute, argument, disputation, disagreement, altercation, contest. Also: feud, fight, quarrel, wrangle, contention
distante — distant (space or time). Also: detached, aloof, standoffish
diálogo — dialogue (form of discourse). Also: dialogue (literary form)
dominio — domain. Also: rule, dominion, dominance, domination, power, control, mastery, sway, ownership
dotación — resource. Also: staff (of workers), array, prize money
dotar — to endow; award. Also: to award
dueño — owner, proprietor, landlord. Also: master
editar — to publish. Also: to edit
embarcación — embarkation, embarkment. Also: ship, watercraft
empatar — to tie, draw (to have the same score or position). Also: to connect, join
empeñar — to pawn, to hock (sell something to a pawn shop). Also: to involve, to pledge, to strive, make an effort, insist
empleo — job, employment. Also: use, application

emprender — to undertake, to engage in. Also: to take (e.g. actions, steps, initiatives, an approach, a path), to start, to launch, to initiate, to begin (e.g. a project, an investigation, a business venture, a negotiation), to set out on, to set forth on (a journey)

encajar — to insert, put into. Also: to fit, to dovetail, to push in, to make sense, add up, be reasonable, to give; to deliver (a hit, punch, kick etc.), to fit in, to fit; squeeze (into), to pull on; slip on (clothes)
encargado — in charge, responsible. Also: entrusted, requested, ordered
encuestar — to survey, interview. Also: to poll
energético — energy, energetic (of or relating to energy).
enganchar — to hook. Also: to get (someone) in one's clutches, to get high (take drugs), to trick or deceive (someone)
enlace — link, connection. Also: bond, relationship, union, marriage, bond, linkage, liaison, go-between, link, hyperlink
enredo — confusion, chaos. Also: machination, wrap, skirt
entrega — delivery, handover. Also: submission, installment, surrender, devotion
erguir — to elevate, lift up, erect. Also: to tower over, stand
escasez — shortage, scarcity, paucity, dearth, scarceness, scantiness.
escoba — broom (domestic utensil). Also: Spanish broom, broom (bush of the genus Retama)
escritorio — writing desk. Also: study (room where writing is done), desktop, desktop computer (the main graphical user interface of an operating system)
escurrir — to drain, to strain. Also: to wring, to slip away, escape
esforzar — to encourage. Also: to strain, struggle, to strive, to attempt
espeso — dense, thick. Also: annoying, bothersome, inappropriate, tiresome, complicated, difficult, causing conflictedness
estadística — statistics (the mathematical science). Also: statistic, female equivalent of estadístico (“statistician”)
estirar — to stretch. Also: to extend
estrenar — to use for the first time (in general). Also: to wear for the first time (for clothing), to premiere, to premiere, to do something for the first time
estética — aesthetics. Also: hair salon, beauty parlor
etcétera — et cetera, and so on.
evaluar — to evaluate. Also: to assess
evidenciar — to demonstrate. Also: to evidence, to evince
exigencia — requirement. Also: demand, need, lack, request
exitoso — successful (achieving or having achieved success). Also: prosperous, thriving (marked by success or prosperity)
expansión — expansion, expanse, spread (extending). Also: expansion, growth, boom, sprawl, sprawling (urban), broadening, expanding (of consciousness or awareness), expansion pack
expectativa — expectation (that which is expected or looked for).
explosión — explosion, blast, bang, burst, boom. Also: bang, boom
expulsar — to expel; to expulse. Also: to vote out, eliminate, to send off, dismiss (show someone a red card)
extinguir — to extinguish, to put out. Also: to carry out (a task, etc.), to go extinct, to die out

fibra — fiber / fibre (a single elongated piece of material). Also: fiber / fibre (a material (especially synthetic) in the form of fibers), fiber / fibre; dietary fiber / dietary fibre, fiberglass / fibreglass
fiera — wild animal, beast. Also: firecracker, spitfire, dragon (a feisty and aggressive woman with a lot of energy)
fijar — to fix, fasten, set, establish, determine. Also: to determine, ascertain, to pin, to notice, To cheat on (a test)
filo — edge, cutting edge (of the blade of an instrument). Also: edge (sharp terminating border), hunger, fold
filtro — love potion, philter. Also: philtrum
finalizar — to finalize, finish. Also: to end
firme — firm, steady, secure. Also: steadfast, unwavering, unswerving, firm, adamant, strong, assertive (uncompromising, unyielding), strong (promising), solid, firm
flequillo — fringe, bangs. Also: diminutive of fleco
flexionar — to bend (knees, legs). Also: to flex (muscles)
foco — spotlight. Also: focus (place or spot where something is focused), light bulb, headlight, flashlight; torch, eyes, shame, Man with hair a little longer than a buzz cut.
fotografiar — to photograph, take a photograph.
fracaso — downfall, ruin, calamity, disaster. Also: failure, flop
frenar — to brake (a vehicle). Also: to restrain
frenético — frenetic, frantic, frenzied. Also: fast-paced
futbolista — footballer (British); football player (Britain), soccer player (US, Canada, Australia).
fútbol — football (soccer). Also: synonym of fútbol americano
ganadería — stockbreeding, animal husbandry, livestock farming, cattle raising, ranching, cattle breeding, livestock raising. Also: livestock
genio — genius. Also: temper, mood (normally a bad temper)
gestionar — to manage. Also: to prosecute, to negotiate, to file (commit official papers to some office)
gestor — manager, overseer. Also: solicitor, attorney, barrister
giro — turn, spin. Also: tour, giro, a money transfer, economical activity type or kind, spin number, turn of events, development, turn of phrase
gitano — Gypsy (of or like a Gypsy).
grifo — tap, faucet (device used to dispense liquids). Also: petrol station, garage, filling station, gas station, service station, griffin

hacha — a kind of torch or large candle (often with four sticks). Also: a kind of wick or fuse (often made with esparto grass and tar), which does not go out easily in the wind, bundle of straw tied up like a strip and often used to help cover huts or other field constructions
hada — fairy. Also: one of the Fates
hallar — to find something, deliberately or not. Also: to strike (a balance between options), to find oneself in a certain way (+adjective), to enjoy oneself somewhere, enjoy one's time
hemisferio — hemisphere (half of the celestial sphere). Also: hemisphere (half of the Earth), hemisphere (half of any sphere)
herir — to wound, injure (to cause physical injury and pain). Also: to hurt, offend, wound (to cause emotional pain)
hermanastro — stepbrother (stepsister for the feminine form), stepsibling (gender neutral term). Also: half brother (half sister for the feminine form)
hormiga — ant (insect of the family Formicidae).
hueco — hollow, empty. Also: gay, clueless

hueso — bone. Also: stone, pit (the central part of some fruits, consisting of the seed and a hard endocarp layer (like those of peach, olive, avocado or mango)), political position (appointed or elected), strict person
idear — to devise, to come up with.
impacto — impact, brunt. Also: hit (e.g., bullets, artillery)
impartir — to impart. Also: to give (classes, lessons), to give, give out (orders)
implantar — to implant. Also: to pass (a law), to introduce oneself
imprenta — printing (the technology). Also: printing press, printer (a factory where printing is done)

impulsar — to impel, to propel. Also: to push, to push for, to push forward, to drive, to propel, to force, to drive forward, to boost, to promote, to jump-start, to drive, to motivate, encourage, to impel
inclinar — to tilt. Also: to bow, to lean (physically), to be inclined
incrementar — to increment, to increase, to rise.
indeciso — indecisive, irresolute, wavering, uncertain, hesitant. Also: undecided, unsettled, unresolved
independizar — to make independent. Also: to become independent
infinidad — infinity. Also: abundance; shedload; a lot
inflación — inflation (expansion or increase in size).

ingenio — genius, wit (faculty to reason and invent quickly). Also: intuition, creative faculty, skill, cleverness, sense of humor, machine, device, war machine, mill, esp. a sugar mill, instrument used by bookbinders to cut the edge of books
ingreso — entrance, ingress. Also: income, deposit
innovador — innovative (characterized by the creation of new ideas or inventions).
inquieto — restless, agitated, unquiet, cross. Also: anxious, worried
inquilino — tenant, renter, lessee. Also: tenant farmer
interacción — interaction (the situation or occurrence in which two or more objects or events act upon one another to produce a new effect).
intimidar — to intimidate, to frighten. Also: to bully
involucrar — to involve. Also: to meddle
jabalí — wild boar. Also: peccary, javelina, warthog (ellipsis of jabalí verrugoso)
jubilación — retirement. Also: pension, retirement pension, old age pension
juicio — good judgement; reason. Also: opinion, judgment, judginess, judgmentalism, trial, verdict
junta — board, council, committee. Also: joint, gasket, meeting (a gathering for a purpose), contact, acquaintances
juzgar — to judge, to adjudicate. Also: to try (a case), to adjudicate, to consider, to pass judgment
lamentar — to lament, to regret. Also: to mourn (often followed by la muerte de or la pérdida de)
latín — Latin (the language of the ancient Romans).

lección — lesson (a section of learning or teaching). Also: lesson (something to learn or be learned), lesson (something that serves as a warning or encouragement), lesson (a section of a religious text)
lema — motto. Also: slogan (especially a political one), theme, subject, headword, term, title, lemma
leve — mild, slight, light. Also: minor, trivial
liar — to bind, tie. Also: to wrap, wrap up, to roll (a cigarette), to deceive, confuse, to french, snog, make out, hook up
liso — smooth. Also: plain, flat, flat-chested (woman), straight
localidad — locality. Also: place, seat (in theatre, show)
logro — attainment, achievement, accomplishment, success. Also: achievement
lucir — to shine. Also: to sport, wear, to show off, to display, to look like
lógica — logic. Also: rationale, logic, female equivalent of lógico (“logician”)
madero — a piece of wood, a piece of timber. Also: police officer, pig, clumsy person
madrugada — dawn. Also: early hours of the morning, before dawn; the period of time between midnight and sunrise
magia — magic. Also: spell, charm, conjuration, wizardry (skill, ability)
majo — kind, friendly, likeable, nice, pretty. Also: good-looking, handsome, said of a person, neither ugly nor very pretty. Sometimes a euphemism for not calling someone "ugly".
manchar — to spot, stain, mark, to smudge. Also: to sully, to tarnish, to taint, to soil, to besmirch, to smear (one's name, reputation, honour, spirit, etc.), to deflower
marcador — scoreboard. Also: bookmark, marker (a gene or DNA sequence with a known location on a chromosome), marker (a felt-tipped pen)
matricular — to enroll, to register, to matriculate.
mediador — mediator (one who negotiates between parties seeking mutual agreement).
mejora — improvement, improving, amelioration, betterment, refinement. Also: enhancement, enhancing, upgrade, upgrading (in quality), upturn
melena — mane (longer hair growth around head of male lions).
mercader — merchant (person who traffics in commodities for profit).
mercancía — ware, merchandise. Also: goods, wares (commodity offered for sale; an article of commerce)
microondas — microwave oven, microwave. Also: necklacing (execution by burning tyre)
minoría — minority (subgroup that does not form half or more). Also: minority (state of being a minor)
mitología — mythology (collection and study of myths).
modernizar — to modernize (to make something modern).
morar — to reside; to dwell; to abide. Also: to sojourn, to indwell (+ en)
morder — to bite, nip. Also: to gnaw, to grasp, clutch, to wear away, to wear down, to corrode (i.e., acid on metal), to criticize, run down, gossip about, find fault with
negociar — to negotiate. Also: to trade, to make money, make profit
notable — remarkable, notable, noteworthy, noticeable, significant, marked, outstanding, striking, noted.
nórdico — Norse (from or native to the Nordic lands). Also: Nordic (pertaining to Nordic languages)
obsesionar — to obsess. Also: to fixate, to fixate on (+con)
ocupación — occupation (the act of possessing a place). Also: occupation (the control of a region by a hostile army), occupation (activity or task with which one occupies oneself)
olvido — oblivion. Also: forgetting, ending of memory, idea meaning an abstract place to put what is wanted to be forgotten, as in the collocation consign to oblivion
onda — wave (on the surface of a liquid). Also: any other wave (including technological use), style; fashion, vibe or mood, bag; cup of tea (one’s personal preference)
operación — operation (a surgical procedure). Also: operation (the method or practice by which actions are done), operation (a procedure for generating a value from one or more other values)
oposición — opposition. Also: a civil service entrance examination
oración — oration. Also: sentence, clause, prayer
orientación — orientation (all senses). Also: orienteering, guidance
orientar — to orient. Also: to get one's bearings, to be directed, to aim, to point at
originar — to originate. Also: to start, to give rise to, to cause
pactar — to agree upon. Also: To make an agreement or contract
parador — inn, hotel. Also: a kind of official luxury hotel, roadside restaurant, rest stop
parecer — to seem, to see fit. Also: to look like, to seem like, to believe, to think, to strike (seem, appear, make an impression), to be like, to take after, to resemble, to perceive
particular — specific, particular. Also: peculiar, strange, personal, private
partida — departure. Also: match, lot, game (of cards, video games etc.), passing (death), certificate
pastilla — pill. Also: cake (block of a dense material), bar (block of soap or chocolate), cube (cube of stock), pad (brake pad)
pastor — shepherd. Also: herder, pastor, priest
patín — skate, roller skate. Also: foul smell, party
peaje — toll (fee for using roads or bridges).

pedazo — piece, chunk, hunk, bit, bite (a part of a larger whole). Also: patch (e.g. of sky, cloth, skin, space), scrap (e.g. of paper, information), lump (e.g. of metal, rock, clay, wood), slab (e.g. of meat, of cheese), shard (e.g. of glass, ice), augmentative of pedo

pelear — to fight (to contend in physical conflict). Also: to fight, to argue, to fight for, to struggle against, to mutually fight, to discord or dispute; to become enemies, to lose or stop, temporally or definitively, a friendship, partnership, etc.
perjudicar — to damage, harm, injure. Also: to malign, defame
perturbar — to perturb, to disturb, to bother.
pinta — look, appearance. Also: small spot, dot or stain, jail, fancy outfit, elegant clothing
pirata — pirate (person who commits robbery at sea against other ships). Also: pirate (person who reproduces copyrighted works without permission), English, British
pizarra — slate (metamorphic rock). Also: blackboard, chalkboard, whiteboard, scoreboard, dashboard (panel with instruments or controls facing the driver in a vehicle)
plantilla — template, stencil. Also: staff, personnel, squad, insole (for the shoe), sole (of tights)

plataforma — platform (a raised stage used for speeches and performances). Also: platform (a place or opportunity to express one's opinions), platform (a particular type of operating system or environment)
portugués — Portuguese (from, native to, or pertaining to Portugal).
practicante — practitioner, practiser. Also: intern, trainee
preservar — to preserve. Also: to be preserved
presionar — to pressure. Also: to push, press, hit, press down (e.g. a button, key, pen), to pressurize, press on (e.g. press on a wound), to urge, push, dragoon, to lobby, to be pressed
prestación — service. Also: perk, benefit, performance, loan, lending
prestigio — prestige, standing, (good) reputation, good name. Also: cachet
presumir — to presume. Also: to boast, to brag, to show off
pretender — to intend, to aim (for/to). Also: to woo, to court, to pretend, purport (claim, allege)
prioridad — priority. Also: right of way
problemático — problematic, problem, troublesome, troubled, problematical.
procurar — to try, to attempt, to endeavor. Also: to make sure, to assure, to obtain, to acquire, to work as a procurator (attorney or agent), to secure
promocionar — to promote, to publicise, to plug.
propiciar — to bring about, enable. Also: to propitiate, appease
proporcionar — to provide, supply. Also: to adapt, adjust, to proportion, set or render in proportion, to get one's hands on
prototipo — prototype (an original object which is a basis for others). Also: paragon, epitome
provecho — benefit, profit. Also: advantage, worth, worthwhileness
proveedor — supplier, provider, purveyor (one who supplies or provides what is needed, especially for subsistence).
proyectar — to project, plan. Also: to show, to shoot, discharge
pudrir — to rot. Also: to rot, to decompose, to waste away, to putrefy
pánico — pertaining to the god Pan. Also: panic (of fear, fright etc.)

quebrar — to break, to snap, to shatter (to smash or rupture). Also: to break (often in legal contexts), to bend, flex or twist, to fail; to go broke; to become bankrupt; to go out of business; to crash, to go bust, to go under (used in political, economic and business/financial/investment contexts), to break up, to kill, to crack, to break, to shatter (putting the onus on the thing cracking itself, absolving any sentient subject of responsibility), to crack, to break (e.g. crack under pressure, break after interrogation), to vomit after drinking too much alcohol
rasgar — to rend, tear, rip. Also: to scratch
raíz — root; root vegetable. Also: root (of a tooth, a nail, etc), root, origin

realización — realization, actualization (process of making something real, act of becoming real or concrete). Also: implementation, conducting, carrying out, execution (e.g. of a project or of measures), completion, fulfillment, achievement, making (e.g. making of a purchase, a flight, a trip, a visit), performance
rebajar — to reduce. Also: to water down, dilute
rebaño — flock. Also: flock, herd (livestock), flock, herd (conformist)
rechazo — refusal. Also: rejection, recoil (of a gun)
recobrar — to recover, regain. Also: to recuperate
recogida — pickup. Also: harvesting, picking, collection (activity of collecting)
redacción — essay, composition. Also: drafting (the act of writing), editing room
redactar — to write up. Also: to draft (a document, law, letter, etc)
redondo — round (circular, cylindrical, or spherical). Also: round (of a number, without fractions), complete, successful, profitable
reducción — reduction. Also: in Spanish America, a settlement created to assimilate and convert the indigenous population
referente — referent. Also: role model, idol, inspiration (someone to look up to and emulate)
reforma — reform (the change of something that is defective, broken, inefficient or otherwise negative).
reforzar — to reinforce, to bolster, to buttress. Also: to strengthen, to increase, to beef up (the level, strength, amount of), to tighten, to tighten up (e.g. security, regulations)

registro — registration, record. Also: roll, register, logbook, log, entry (in a register or log), search (of a building, by police etc.), recording (of audio or video), register, register (of voice), record; personal best
rehuir — to avoid. Also: to shun, shrink, retreat (withdraw or retire, as from danger)
reinar — to reign; to rule over.
reja — grate, grating, grille. Also: bars (especially of a prison cell)
remar — to paddle. Also: to row
remitir — to remit, pardon. Also: to send, transfer, to refer, to hand down, to die down; die out (lose intensity)
renacentista — Renaissance; of or relating to the Renaissance.
renovar — to renovate. Also: to renew
renta — income. Also: rent, protection money
reparación — reparation. Also: the act or result of repairing; repair, repairs, comeback
representativo — representative (which represents someone or something).
requisito — requirement, prerequisite, condition, precondition. Also: requisite
resaltar — to bounce. Also: to chip off, to break off, to stick out, to jut out (to protrude, to extend beyond), to stand out, to stick out (to be extraordinary), to emphasize, to highlight
respecto — respect, regard, connection, relation, relationship.
restar — to subtract, to reduce, to deduct. Also: to remain, to return (hit the ball back after a serve)
restauración — restoration, reconstruction. Also: restoration (political), restaurateurship, restaurant-management
reto — challenge (a difficult task). Also: dare, scolding; insult
retrasar — to delay, to set back, to slow down, to postpone, to retard, to hold up, to put back.
revelar — to reveal. Also: to develop (to bring out images latent in photographic film)
rodilla — knee (joint or region of the joint in the middle part of the leg between the thigh and the shank).
romance — romance, love affair. Also: romance (genre), novel
rondar — to patrol. Also: to go around, round, go round, to frequent, hang out, to get together (to make music), to hang around, to be around, be roughly, be close to
rostro — face (the front part of the head).
ruptura — rupture. Also: breaking off; breakup
sacrificar — to sacrifice. Also: to put down, euthanize (an animal)
semejante — similar. Also: such, what a (when followed by a noun)
servidor — server. Also: yours truly; muggins
señar — to sign (to use sign language).
silvestre — wild (untamed; not domesticated). Also: uncultivated, rustic, rural
soberbio — haughty. Also: arrogant, angry, superb
sobrar — to be superfluous; to be left over or extra.
sociólogo — sociologist (scientist studying the field of sociology).
solicitud — request, solicitation. Also: survey, application (a verbal or written request for assistance or employment or admission to a school, course or similar)
someter — to subdue, quell, conquer. Also: to subject to, to subordinate (put under the control of), to submit, put forward (an opinion), to undergo, to be subjected to (+ a)
soporte — support, strut, pillar. Also: mount, mounting, stand, holder, bracket (to support shelves), medium, kickstand, supporter

sostener — to hold, to hold up, to prop up, to sustain, to underpin (physically). Also: to sustain, to support, to keep (i.e. to give continuity to), to support, to uphold, to hold, to sustain, to contend, to maintain, to argue, to claim, to underpin, to defend
sostén — support, prop. Also: bra, brassière, sustenance, nourishment
sublevar — to provoke into rebellion, to revolt. Also: to raise, to lift up
subterráneo — underground (regions beneath the surface of the earth). Also: subway, metro
suceso — event, happening. Also: outcome, result, success
sucursal — branch (of a company or organization). Also: section
sudar — to sweat. Also: to not care
suelto — free, loose, undone. Also: isolated, separate, free, agile, fluent, shaggy, easy (consenting readily to sex)
sujetar — to steady, hold, secure, attach, fasten. Also: to subject
tabla — board, plank. Also: slab, table (grid or matrix, in a book, data base, file, document, etc.), table (furniture), skirt pleat, stage, ability on stage, ability as a public speaker
talón — heel. Also: heel (shoe), check (cheque, a financial instrument)
tarifa — fare (money paid for a transport ticket). Also: fee
teletrabajo — working from home, WFH, telecommuting, telework.
tergiversar — to misrepresent, to twist, to distort. Also: to tergiversate, to prevaricate
tierno — tender, affectionate. Also: soft, delicate, cuddly, tender, young, tender (meat, etc.), green, unripe (vegetables)

tiro — throw (the act of throwing something). Also: shot; gunshot (the result of launching a projectile or bullet), range (the distance from a person or sensor to an object), shooting (the sport or activity of firing a gun or other weapon), shot (the act of launching a ball or similar object toward a goal), team (a set of draught animals), intake of air in a space, inseam (the seam of a trouser up the inside of the leg), fix (dose of a drug)

toque — a touch. Also: a strum, a toll (musical sound used to announce something), assay by touchstone, ellipsis of piedra de toque, application of topical medication, crux, assessment, trial, ellipsis of toque de atención, a punch, a light brushstroke, soft touch (something handled, or requiring handling with the utmost gentleness), ellipsis of toque de santo, toke, puff of marijuana, a blunt, snorting (of cocaine), live concert
torbellino — tourbillon, whirlwind. Also: whirl, whirling, swirl, maelstrom

tornar — to return. Also: to come back; to go back, to put back, to change, to (do) again, to revive (to recover from a state of unconsciousness), to become (e.g. change in a characteristic, nature of something or status)
transcurrir — to pass by, to elapse.
trapo — rag, piece of cloth used for cleaning, washing or drying (tea towel, dish towel, dish cloth, dust cloth, paper towel). Also: any piece of cloth, rag (derogative for a flag), clothing, clothes
trascender — to transcend, to go beyond.
traslado — move, removal. Also: transfer, copy
traspasar — to transfer. Also: to penetrate, to pierce, to violate (rules, laws etc), to overstep (limits)
trastorno — disorder. Also: disturbance (of mind), upheaval
trato — treatment (the process or manner of treating someone or something). Also: deal, bargain, agreement, behaviour
trayectoria — trajectory. Also: sequence of events of a professional career, artistic career, a social group, a war, etc., course, path
trazo — line, outline (writing, design). Also: stroke (writing)
tronco — trunk (tree trunk). Also: trunk (main channel), torso, frustum, team (of mules, etc.)
tropezar — to trip, stumble, bump into. Also: to get tripped up, to meet (someone) by chance
tutor — guardian (person responsible for another). Also: tutor (teacher), trellis
unión — union. Also: unity, togetherness, binding, junction
vacilar — to hesitate, waver, dither (UK), vacillate. Also: to enjoy, to spy on, to banter, kid, tease, rib, trick; to play a joke on, to pull someone's leg
vago — wanderer. Also: vagabond, bum, slacker
vano — vain (overly proud of oneself). Also: vain (having no real substance), vain (pointless, futile)
veneno — poison (substance that is harmful or lethal to a living organism). Also: venom (poison carried by an animal)

villa — small town. Also: villa, settlement with a minimum of five thousand inhabitants (bigger than a town but smaller than a city) that has asked for the title officially; previously, this title was granted by the king, ellipsis of villa miseria (“slum”)
vincular — to bind. Also: to link
vocación — vocation (inclination to undertake a certain kind of work). Also: calling
votar — to vote. Also: to throw
yema — egg yolk. Also: tip (of a finger), bud; shoot
zapata — brake shoe. Also: washer (flat disk placed beneath a nut or at some joint)
ácido — sharp, tart. Also: acidic, harsh
íntimo — intimate. Also: private, familiar, cosy, close

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
